qa_pairs.question: When will the Vulcan cluster be available?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan will be available on April 15, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the login node for the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The login node for Vulcan is vulcan.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Globus collection for Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Globus collection for Vulcan is 'Vulcan Globus v5', accessible via https://app.globus.org/file-manager?origin_id=97bda3da-a723-4dc0-ba7e-728f35183b43.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for the Vulcan System Status Page?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the Vulcan System Status Page is https://status.alliancecan.ca/system/Vulcan.

qa_pairs.question: What is the web address for the Vulcan portal?
qa_pairs.answer: The web address for the Vulcan portal is https://portal.vulcan.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is a cluster dedicated to the needs of the Canadian scientific Artificial Intelligence community.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the Vulcan cluster located?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster is located at the University of Alberta.

qa_pairs.question: Who manages the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is managed by the University of Alberta and Amii.

qa_pairs.question: What is the origin of the name 'Vulcan' for the cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster is named after the town Vulcan, AB, located in southern Alberta.

qa_pairs.question: What larger compute environment is Vulcan a part of?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is part of the Pan-Canadian AI Compute Environment (PAICE).

qa_pairs.question: Is internet access generally available from Vulcan's compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: No, internet access is not generally available from the compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How can users access the internet from compute nodes on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: A globally available Squid proxy is enabled by default with certain domains whitelisted. If you are not able to connect to a domain, you should contact technical support.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum duration for jobs on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum duration of jobs on Vulcan is 7 days.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is currently open to all researchers doing research on AI or applying AI methods in their research.

qa_pairs.question: What do I need to do to log in to Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: To be able to log in to Vulcan, you must request access in CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to submit jobs on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: To be able to submit jobs, you must be a member of an AIP RAP.

qa_pairs.question: If I am a PI and don't have an AIP RAP, what should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a PI and you do not already have an AIP RAP, you should apply for General Access to PAICE Systems.

qa_pairs.question: How can a PI add other researchers to their AIP RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can add other researchers to their AIP RAP by navigating to the "Resource Allocation Projects" table on the CCDB home page, locating the RAPI of their AIP project, clicking on it to reach the RAP management page, clicking "Manage RAP memberships," and then entering the CCRI of the user in the "Add Members" section.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes does the Vulcan cluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster has 252 nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What model are the nodes in the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The nodes in the Vulcan cluster are Dell R760xa.

qa_pairs.question: What CPUs are used in each Vulcan node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node uses 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6448Y CPUs.

qa_pairs.question: How many cores does each Vulcan node possess?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 64 cores.

qa_pairs.question: What is the system memory per node in Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 512 GB of system memory.

qa_pairs.question: What type and quantity of GPUs are installed in each Vulcan node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 4 x NVIDIA L40s 48GB GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total number of GPUs available in the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster has a total of 1008 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What type of storage system does Vulcan use?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan's storage system uses a combination of NVMe flash and HDD storage.

qa_pairs.question: What platform is Vulcan's storage system built upon?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan's storage system runs on the Dell PowerScale platform.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total usable storage capacity of Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan has a total usable storage capacity of approximately 5PB.

qa_pairs.question: Are Home, Scratch, and Project spaces on different storage systems?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Home, Scratch, and Project spaces are all on the same Dell PowerScale system.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Home space on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Home space is the location of /home directories.

qa_pairs.question: Does the Home space have a fixed quota?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each /home directory in the Home space has a small fixed quota.

qa_pairs.question: Is Home space allocated through RAS or RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Home space is not allocated via RAS or RAC; larger requests should use the /project space.

qa_pairs.question: Is data in the Home space backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Home space has daily backup.

qa_pairs.question: What is the intended use of the Scratch space?
qa_pairs.answer: Scratch space is intended for active or temporary storage.

qa_pairs.question: Is Scratch space allocated?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Scratch space is not allocated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the quota policy for Scratch space?
qa_pairs.answer: Scratch space has a large fixed quota per user.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to inactive data in Scratch space?
qa_pairs.answer: Inactive data in Scratch space will be purged.

qa_pairs.question: What type of quota does Project space offer?
qa_pairs.answer: Project space offers a large adjustable quota per project.

qa_pairs.question: Is data in the Project space backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Project space has daily backup.

qa_pairs.question: What type of network interconnect do Vulcan's nodes use?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan's nodes are interconnected with 100Gbps Ethernet with RoCE (RDMA over Converged Ethernet) enabled.

qa_pairs.question: What scheduler does the Vulcan cluster employ?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster uses the Slurm scheduler to run user workloads.

qa_pairs.question: How is software managed on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan uses a module-based software stack.

qa_pairs.question: What kinds of software are available on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Both the standard Alliance software stack and cluster-specific software are available on Vulcan.
